title: Evangelion 10 English dub premieres next month in Texas
display_title: Evangelion 10 English dub premieres next month in Texas
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 1.0 English dub premieres next month in Texas             Quote Post by JupiterCallisto » Wed Jun 03, 2009 4:53 pm This is probaly the reason they got the movie rated by the MPAA. Proof : http://www.activeanime.com/html/content/view/5070/36/ Quick...somebody go and tell us did they edit anything!

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Jun 03, 2009 4:56 pm That's in my city but why the hell would I want to see the dub? Convince me why I should go, EGF.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jun 03, 2009 5:12 pm 1) Telling us if it's decent for an Eva dub. 2) Telling us if anything was edited from the showing.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jun 03, 2009 5:46 pm Well strictly speaking, if they've actually made a print of the film on 35mm and aren't just playing the DVD played in a movie theater you will be seeing the best image quality version of Eva 1.0 on the planet! Far better image quality, depth, and magic than anything Blu-Ray can give you. Even now the best way to see how a movie really looks is on film in a theater. So call the theater and find out if it's on FILM. Even if it is the dub it's a far better way to see it than the rest of non-Japanese located people can. If it's just the DVD blown up large, forget it.

[]:             Quote Post by PuppetChaos » Wed Jun 03, 2009 6:21 pm Well, I'd see it if I lived in Texas. I mean, sure, the dub might suck (but who knows, it might be good! What?!) ...but this is the sort of movie I want to see in a theater. I'm a bit tired of squinting at a computer screen.

[]:             Quote Post by squall.leonhart » Wed Jun 03, 2009 6:39 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Well strictly speaking, if they've actually made a print of the film on 35mm and aren't just playing the DVD played in a movie theater you will be seeing the best image quality version of Eva 1.0 on the planet! Far better image quality, depth, and magic than anything Blu-Ray can give you. Even now the best way to see how a movie really looks is on film in a theater. So call the theater and find out if it's on FILM. Even if it is the dub it's a far better way to see it than the rest of non-Japanese located people can. If it's just the DVD blown up large, forget it.

[]:             Quote Post by The Invincible Shinji » Wed Jun 03, 2009 6:39 pm PuppetChaos wrote: ..but this is the sort of movie I want to see in a theater. I'm a bit tired of squinting at a computer screen.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jun 03, 2009 6:43 pm Arguably yes a film can downgrade over time with scratches (though I have a softness for the minute almost unoticeable ones) and also yes, if the theater is doing a shitty job that can ruin the image quality in a similar manner like a bad digital transfer. BUT- if this showing was on film the print would be so fresh there'd be no scratches.

[]:             Quote Post by squall.leonhart » Wed Jun 03, 2009 6:47 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Arguably yes a film can downgrade over time with scratches (though I have a softness for the minute almost unoticeable ones) and also yes, if the theater is doing a shitty job that can ruin the image quality in a similar manner like a bad digital transfer. BUT- if this showing was on film the print would be so fresh there'd be no scratches.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Wed Jun 03, 2009 6:48 pm AFAIK all current blu-ray releases are 24-bit color, whereas digital projection is 36-bit color (i.e. far better contrast and clarity), and mastering for film is typically done with 48-bit color. And any LCD TV is crap when compared to a professional digital projection system, as far as contrast goes. Blu-ray is not theater quality, unless the theater is screwing something up.

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Wed Jun 03, 2009 7:01 pm WIN! Alamo draft house is literally 10 minutes from where I live. I'll be there.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jun 03, 2009 7:51 pm Something tells me they have the wrong trailer up... At least they're helping fans get excited about 2.0? Though fans that go see 1.0 and went onto that website are gonna be so confused why Asuka and the "new girl" aren't there....

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Wed Jun 03, 2009 8:20 pm I wonder what peoples' response to the dubbed voices will be. I wonder if they will be good.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Jun 03, 2009 8:24 pm I may go see it and write up a review of/thoughts on the dub for the front page. I'm 100% sub fan when it comes to almost anything, but I'll give my honest thoughts.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 03, 2009 9:38 pm Yes, Zap! Take one for the team!

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Jun 03, 2009 9:49 pm I'm still sore from the last time you said that to me.

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Wed Jun 03, 2009 10:13 pm I'm a fan of the dub, so a few of you might find my opinions to be of value. (You know who you are!) I called Alamo Drafthouse today, to see when I could reserve tickets, and the lady I talked to had NO idea what I was talking about. I'm also curious if Spike, Allison, or the director are showing up. Hmm..

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Wed Jun 03, 2009 10:19 pm well with one person for sub and one for dub, we should have a combined "objective" review for it

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Wed Jun 03, 2009 10:23 pm Hey Zap, just rip the original soundtrack and bring a good pair of soundproof headphones. It'll be a unique theater experience...

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Thu Jun 04, 2009 12:38 am You guys who can actually see Evangelion in a theater should consider yourselves friggin' lucky. The closest I might ever be able to hope to get will be possibly screening the Rebuild Blu-Ray at my school. Zap, even if you hate dubs, seeing this in a theater is a once in a life time opportunity that you should not pass up. And as Axell suggested, you and Darkorb can offer us a balanced opinion since one of you likes dubs and the other doesn't. Also, it'd be nice if we can get a confirmation as to whether or not this is actually 1.0, or if by some miracle it's actually 1.11 and Funimation simply mislabeled it.
